title: Fed SLOOS Report — Credit Liquidity Update
desc: The Senior Loan Officer Opinion Survey will reveal if banks are tightening or loosening credit. This is a primary driver for Net Liquidity; a 'tightening' result could stall BTC's move toward $80k as the broader money supply feels the squeeze.

title: ISM Services PMI & RBA Decision
desc: A heavy macro day. Global markets will react to Australia's rate decision before the US Services PMI print. A weak US services sector would strengthen the 'Recession Hedge' case for Gold and Bitcoin as the DXY faces pressure.

title: Coinbase (COIN) Q1 Earnings Report
desc: The 'Vibe Check' for the crypto industry. Analysts are watching for retail volume growth and custodial fee revenue from Spot ETFs. A beat here validates the institutional adoption narrative and often acts as a front-runner for BTC price action.

title: MicroStrategy (MSTR) Earnings & BTC Strategy
desc: Expect Saylor to provide updates on the 'Bitcoin Treasury' model. The market will be watching for any new debt issuance or BTC purchase announcements. MSTR remains the high-beta proxy for institutional Bitcoin sentiment.

title: US Non-Farm Payrolls (NFP)
desc: The most important data point of the week. A 'Goldilocks' report (not too hot, not too cold) is needed to keep the rally alive. If unemployment rises, the market will pivot to 'Rate Cut' mode, likely causing a spike in BTC and ETH volatility.

title: Consumer Sentiment (Univ. of Michigan)
desc: Closing the week with a look at inflation expectations. High expectations here could lead to a 'Hawkish' weekend, potentially capping BTC's gains as traders hedge against a persistent 'higher for longer' Fed stance.

title: Monthly Token Unlock Cycle — Phase 1
desc: The first major cliff for several Tier-1 altcoin projects begins. We watch for potential sell-pressure on the ETH/Alt pairs. High-liquidity days often absorb these unlocks, but thin weekend volumes could create localized price dips.
